explanation.summary: The immutable result selected the Direct Cutover candidate. Both candidates were eligible and passed every gate twice, including integrity, ML-DSA verification, tamper rejection, context binding, wrong-key rejection, legacy-policy evaluation, and repeatability. The selected implementation replaces RSA-SHA256 release sealing with native node:crypto ML-DSA-65, binds signatures to the required context, emits envelope version 2, changes only src/release-seal.cjs, and adds no dependencies.
explanation.whySelected: Direct Cutover satisfied the declared constraint that legacy compatibility was not required while producing 0 RSA signatures, compared with 1 for Compatibility Bridge. It also had the smaller measured envelope (4608 bytes versus 4971) and fewer changed lines (10 versus 11). These immutable gate outcomes and measurements support the recorded deterministic selection of direct; GPT did not alter the selection.
limitations: This explanation does not modify or reinterpret the recorded selection, gates, diffs, or measurements.
The result applies to repository commit 9cc00b69982ea5d0782ff5bf68267ba92d33311e under the recorded configuration, contract, runtime, evaluator, and hashes.
Key origin, storage, rotation, and external exposure were not established by the scanner evidence.
Legacy RSA consumers are intentionally unsupported by the selected candidate because legacyCompatibilityRequired was false.
The recorded verifyMedianMs and verifyP95Ms are both 0; no greater precision or alternative performance claim can be inferred from this result.
